channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canadian Soldiers Rescue Abandoned Baby in England During WWII
item.description: On September 23, 1941, a newborn baby was abandoned in Horsell Common, a park near London. Three Canadian soldiers—Gunner Robert C. Griffin from Regina, Saskatchewan, Lance Sergeant Ernest Curtis from...

item.title: Researchers Identify First AI-Generated Zero-Day Exploit Amid Rising Cybersecurity Risks
item.description: At the end of 2025, researchers found that widely available AI tools, often used for productivity, can also be misused for cyberattacks. These tools make it easier for attackers to write sophisticated...

item.title: India raises import duties on gold and silver amid rupee decline
item.description: India has decided to increase import duties on gold and silver significantly, aiming to protect its weakening rupee and support foreign currency reserves. The government more than doubled taxes from 6...

item.title: Labour Party in Nasarawa Elects Asoloko Maku as State Chairman Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Labour Party for Nasarawa State don elect new chairman, Asoloko Maku, wey go lead the party as dem dey prepare for 2027 elections. For press briefing wey happen for Lafia, Maku talk say the recent con...

item.title: The Life and Legacy of Soraya Esfandiary-Bakhtiary: Queen of Iran and European Socialite
item.description: Soraya Esfandiary-Bakhtiary (1932–2001) was the second wife of Shah Mohammad Reza Pahlavi, serving as Queen of Iran from 1951 until their divorce in 1958. Her marriage, though initially celebrated, fa...

item.title: BNXN and Sarz drop five-track EP 'The Game Needs Us', climbs Apple Music Nigeria charts
item.description: Naija music fans don get reason to dey ginger as BNXN and Sarz don release their joint EP, 'The Game Needs Us'. The EP get five tracks and e land for Apple Music Nigeria Top Albums chart at number two...

item.title: Overview of BusinessDay E-Edition for 13th May 2026
item.description: This article na the digital edition of BusinessDay newspaper for Wednesday, 13th May 2026. E no really get in-depth news content but e dey provide access to different sections of the newspaper like Ta...

item.title: The Life and Art of Charles Spencelayh: Victorian and Edwardian Genre Painter
item.description: Charles Spencelayh (1865–1958) was an English painter renowned for his meticulous genre paintings, capturing the intricate details of everyday life. Often referred to as a 'microscopic' realist, he sp...

item.title: Shirley Anne Field: Celebrating the Legacy of a British Cinema Star
item.description: Shirley Anne Field (1936–2023) was a prominent figure in British cinema, particularly known for her influence during the 'New Wave' movement of the late 1950s and 1960s. Originally a successful photog...

item.title: News Corp Australia website access blocked for suspected automated traffic
item.description: The article explains that users trying to access certain News Corp Australia web pages may be blocked if the website detects automated traffic, such as crawler bots. This is done using specialised sof...

item.title: Researchers Identify Key Molecular Mechanism Used by Enteroviruses for Replication
item.description: Scientists at the University of Maryland, Baltimore County, have revealed a critical mechanism that enteroviruses, including polio and the common cold virus, use to replicate inside human cells. The s...

item.title: Suvendu Adhikari Retains Bhabanipur Seat, Vacates Nandigram After Election Victory
item.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari, who recently won in both the Bhabanipur and Nandigram Assembly constituencies, has decided to retain the Bhabanipur seat and give up the Nandigram seat. On...

item.title: Helen Zille challenges Johannesburg over mismanagement of water services
item.description: Helen Zille, the DA's mayoral candidate, has approached the Johannesburg High Court, taking on the City of Johannesburg and Joburg Water over what she describes as a 'man-made' water crisis. The DA al...

item.title: Alfred and Yasmine Vargas Advocate for Anti-Bullying Awareness
item.description: Alfred Vargas, actor and public servant, along with his wife Yasmine, is actively campaigning against bullying in the Philippines. Through their advocacy, they aim to raise awareness and encourage vic...

item.title: Trump Administration Pursues Aggressive Voter Roll Purges Ahead of 2026 Elections
item.description: According to reporting highlighted by Raw Story, the Trump administration is undertaking a nationwide effort to remove tens of millions of voters from registration rolls, targeting primarily Democrati...

item.title: Senate Confirms No Arrests Will Be Made Amid Ongoing Investigation
item.description: On May 13, 2026, the Office of the Senate Affairs (OSAA) clarified that no arrests will be conducted within the Senate premises despite ongoing investigations into certain matters. The statement came ...

item.title: Constitutional Court Rules Against Multiple Asylum Applications in South Africa
item.description: The South African Department of Home Affairs has achieved a major legal victory in its efforts to prevent abuse of the asylum system. The Constitutional Court, in the case of Director-General, Departm...

item.title: xAI Expands Gas Turbine Use at Southaven Data Center Amid Environmental Lawsuit
item.description: xAI has installed 19 additional portable natural gas turbines at its Colossus 2 data center in Southaven, Mississippi, raising concerns over air quality and regulatory compliance. Internal emails obta...

item.title: Lee Lai’s Graphic Novel Cannon Wins the 2026 Stella Prize
item.description: Lee Lai has made history as the first graphic novelist to win the 2026 Stella Prize with her work Cannon. Previously shortlisted in 2022 for Stone Fruit, Lai has contributed to prestigious publication...

item.title: Senator Bato dela Rosa Under Protective Custody Amid ICC Warrant
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa is currently under protective custody within the Senate compound following the issuance of an International Criminal Court (ICC) arrest warrant li...
